florr.io client research

A visual browser for everything extracted from the modern florr.io client, a 9 MB Rust to WebAssembly binary. Content model, vector art, and map grids, decoded straight from the binary's data section.
Disclaimer. This page and its write-up were generated by AI from a static read of the game's WebAssembly binary. The information it displays is human-made: every petal, mob, talent, name, description, sprite, and map grid shown here was created by the developers of florr.io and is merely surfaced here. Treat this as a best-effort reconstruction, not official documentation. Localized text in the binary is mixed across languages, so some names/descriptions may appear in a language other than English. Nothing here is affiliated with or endorsed by the developer.
Extraction / repo winuser3661 florr.io & original assets Matheus Valadares (M28) Join the Discord

Overview

The client ships as a single stripped WebAssembly module. Symbol names are gone and the imports/exports are minified, so there is no clean decompile, but the 4 MB data section still leaks the entire content model and every embedded asset.

Petals

Mobs

Talents

Rarities

Ten tiers, from common up to the craft-only and event tiers.

Maps and biomes

Achievements

Vector assets

The binary has no id-to-sprite table, so entity names below are best-effort visual identifications (a trailing "?" means uncertain). Assets are numbered in binary order.

Misc

Terrain, biome & structure tiles

Raw source SVGs

Every raw petal, mob, and talent SVG collected (florr.io asset ids plus named files), dumped here as-is.

Map grids

Each map's terrain/tile grid, decoded from an embedded gzip blob. Square grids are rendered as a grayscale image (byte value scaled for contrast); non-square grids show dimensions only.

Changelog

The in-game changelog, embedded verbatim in the binary.

    CDN paths

    Everything above is pulled out of the binary offline. This is where the running client actually fetches its files at load time — florr.io's versioned static host. Paths are pinned to one build (the hash below); each file links to the live URL.

    base = https://static.florr.io//

    Numbered petal/mob/talent SVGs sit at the hash root; everything else lives under the static/ subtree (the un-prefixed path 404s). No audio, no fonts beyond Ubuntu-B.ttf, no source maps, and no directory listing exist — only the 6 locales shown.

    Generated by AI from clientnew.wasm. Displayed information is the work of the florr.io developers. Repo and extraction by winuser3661; florr.io and its original assets by Matheus Valadares (M28). Community: discord.gg/4w4D3mNNtf.
